claim: Files are canonical, the graph is a derived and regenerable index — confirmed by BasicMemory, Palinode, LightRAG, and Obsidian independently
evidence: All four production systems studied make the same architectural choice: plain files are the authoritative source of truth and the graph/database is a derived index that can be rebuilt from files

claim: The @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory MCP server is insufficient for T28: it uses JSONL flat file with full rewrites per operation, no FTS, no vector, no graph traversal
evidence: Direct codebase analysis: every operation deserializes entire file, every mutation rewrites entirely, search is O(N x observations) substring scan. GitHub Issue #2415 documents degradation at ~80K tokens

claim: SQLite + simple-graph schema (nodes + edges tables with JSON columns) is adequate for T28's scale of several thousand nodes and 5000+ edges
evidence: Pattern used by simple-graph, code-review-graph, codebase-memory-mcp. Comfortable to ~100K nodes/edges. Multiple MCP wrappers confirmed (mcp-server-sqlite, code-review-graph, codebase-memory-mcp)

claim: SQLite FTS5 + sqlite-vec enables hybrid keyword+vector search on the same SQLite database, no external infrastructure required
evidence: FTS5 is built-in SQLite, BM25-scored, handles ~500K docs. sqlite-vec is a well-maintained extension. Combination demonstrated in obra/knowledge-graph and codebase-memory-mcp

claim: KuzuDB (original) was archived in October 2025 when the team was acquired — it is not a viable backend choice
evidence: D1b finding: KuzuDB archived October 2025, team acquired, likely by Apple. D1a's recommendation for kuzu-memory applies to LadybugDB (the community fork) not the original package

claim: LadybugDB is the strongest embedded graph-native option: MIT, v0.15.3 (April 2026), 894 stars, Windows x64 support, Cypher, native FTS+vector, npm package
evidence: Community fork of KuzuDB with 894 stars, @ladybugdb/core npm package, cross-platform including Windows x64, openCypher, ACID, larger-than-memory support. Active Discord and April 2026 releases

claim: LadybugDB is 6 months old as a community fork — production stability is unverified and should be reassessed at 12 months (October 2026)
evidence: Fork started after KuzuDB archival in October 2025. 6 months of community fork history is insufficient to assess production hardening for critical data

claim: DuckDB is OLAP (columnar) with 10-500x write penalty for OLTP point inserts vs SQLite — wrong fit for T28's incremental growth pattern
evidence: DuckDB design is columnar for analytical scans. T28's ingestion is OLTP-style incremental inserts. 10-500x write slowdown confirmed in D1a findings. Appropriate only as read-only analytical layer

claim: BasicMemory's architecture (Markdown files + SQLite index + FastEmbed + MCP-native) is the closest zero-infrastructure match to T28's requirements
evidence: AGPL v3 licensed. Primary interface is MCP (write_note, read_note, build_context with memory:// URL graph traversal, search). Hybrid FTS+vector in v0.19.0. Zero infrastructure

claim: BasicMemory's AGPL v3 license constrains direct adoption as T28's backend codebase — adopt the architecture pattern without the codebase
evidence: AGPL v3 requires derivative works to be open-sourced under same terms. T28 as part of JASON-OS (a personal Claude Code OS) would be affected

claim: Graphiti/Zep's architectural patterns are best-in-class for T28's schema design: bi-temporal edges, three-tier hierarchy, hybrid RRF retrieval, non-destructive contradiction
evidence: arXiv:2501.13956, 24,600+ stars, Apache 2.0. Bi-temporal modeling (t_valid, t_created/t_expired), Episodes->Entities->Communities hierarchy, hybrid retrieval via Reciprocal Rank Fusion

claim: Graphiti/Zep requires LLM API per ingestion and cannot run fully offline — this creates cost and availability constraints for T28
evidence: Graph building requires LLM API calls for entity extraction and edge creation. No offline mode. Backends require Neo4j, FalkorDB, Kuzu, or Neptune — all server-class infrastructure

claim: The three-tier schema (SourceNode -> KnowledgeNode -> ThemeNode) with six edge types is the correct schema for T28
evidence: Validated against Graphiti (Episodes->Entities->Communities), MAGMA (multi-graph decomposition), A-MEM (multi-attribute nodes). Goldilocks ranges: 3-7 node types, 5-15 edge types

claim: The T28 graph must be reconstructible from .research/ file artifacts alone via a rebuild command — compaction resilience is non-negotiable
evidence: Claude Code compacts regularly. Windows background agent output files are 0 bytes (known bug anthropics/claude-code#39791). Graph backend (SQLite DB, MCP server) loss must be recoverable

claim: The existing analysis.json schema 4.3 files map directly to SourceNode properties with no transformation required
evidence: Each analysis.json has slug, stars, language, repoType, repoSubtype, analyzedAt — all map directly to SourceNode base properties. Direct JSON read, no ETL needed

claim: EXTRACTIONS.md is already a derived view of extraction-journal.jsonl — the project already uses the file-canonical/derived-view pattern
evidence: EXTRACTIONS.md auto-generates from extraction-journal.jsonl. The graph formalizes and extends what the project is already doing instinctively

claim: Ingest and absorb must be decoupled operations: ingest is metadata registration, absorb is semantic extraction
evidence: Rate limits, paywalls, async fetches require separating source registration from entity/relation extraction. Coupled operations block on external dependencies

claim: Anti-cramming threshold: split node when >3 semantically distinct edge clusters, or >8 observations per node, or contradictory incoming edges
evidence: Derived from farzaa architecture analysis in codebase prior art. 15-entry checkpoint cycle: dedup + cramming/thinning checks + index rebuild after every 15 absorb operations

claim: Synthesis nodes should compound into a living wiki: each synthesis result becomes a node linked to its sources, ranking higher than raw sources on repeated retrieval
evidence: Derived from Karpathy and A-MEM bidirectional evolution patterns. Uses A-MEM's mechanism: new node insertion triggers regeneration of linked nodes' contextual attributes

claim: The term 'Dual-Embedding Memory Bank' does not appear in MemSkill (arXiv:2602.02474) — the mechanism is a single shared embedding space
evidence: Direct paper review: MemSkill uses flat memory items (Trace-Specific Memory Bank + Skill Bank), single shared embedding space (Contriever). No dual-embedding architecture described

claim: MemSkill is not a graph schema reference — it uses flat memory items. Graphiti and MAGMA are the applicable T28 schema references
evidence: MemSkill's data model: Entity{name, entityType, observations[]} and Relation{from, to, relationType}. No entity/relation typed graph schema. Brainstorm decision #9 needs correction

claim: AgentMemory (574 stars) syncs with ~/.claude/projects/*/memory/MEMORY.md — directly compatible with this project's existing auto-memory infrastructure
evidence: D5b finding: AgentMemory 43 MCP tools, triple-stream retrieval (BM25 + vector + knowledge graph BFS), supersession chains with rollback. MEMORY.md sync path confirmed in documentation

claim: obra/knowledge-graph provides the strongest graph analytics (PageRank, Louvain, betweenness centrality, BFS) in a serverless Claude Code plugin format
evidence: 55 stars (low adoption), released March 2026. Exports Obsidian vault to SQLite+sqlite-vec+FTS5+graphology. Local MiniLM model (no API key). Designed as Claude Code plugin with auto-start MCP

claim: MemoryGraph (202 stars) supports LadybugDB as one of 8 backend options — the only confirmed MCP server with LadybugDB support
evidence: D5b finding: MemoryGraph has 8 backend options including LadybugDB, 7 relationship categories with semantic types, cross-session recall, designed for AI coding agents

claim: mind-mem implements contradiction detection as a first-class feature — surfaces when stored knowledge conflicts with new knowledge
evidence: Brand new (March 2026, 10 stars). Co-retrieval graph (PageRank-style score propagation across frequently co-retrieved blocks). SQLite+Markdown, zero deps. 2,189 unit tests

claim: Only 2 serverless MCP servers offer graph analytics (PageRank, community detection, centrality): obra/knowledge-graph and neo4j-contrib/mcp-neo4j (via GDS)
evidence: D5a survey finding. All other serverless MCP servers provide keyword or vector search but not structural graph algorithms

claim: SQLite .db binary files should be gitignored; .research/ markdown and JSON files track normally — this is the correct git pattern for T28
evidence: BasicMemory, Palinode, and Obsidian all gitignore the database file and track the source files. SQLite binary generates meaningless diffs

claim: Knowledge networks self-organize into scale-free distributions (alpha~3.0) with small-world properties (avg path 4.8-5.2) — bridge nodes appear linearly without saturation
evidence: D10 academic finding. Scale-free emergence is referenced from graph theory literature but specific constants (alpha=3.0, path 4.8-5.2) are cited without primary source in D10

claim: GraphRAG achieves 72-83% win rate over vector RAG on comprehensiveness (EMNLP 2025) — graph-augmented retrieval consistently outperforms vector-only on complex queries
evidence: Single paper claim from D10. Self-reported by GraphRAG authors. Not independently replicated. Percentage range suggests multiple evaluation configurations

claim: Graphiti achieves 18.5% accuracy gain and 90% latency reduction on LongMemEval vs full-context baseline
evidence: Self-reported by Zep/Graphiti authors in arXiv:2501.13956. LongMemEval is a specific benchmark. Independent replication not found

claim: Multiple MCP servers claim #1 on LongMemEval/LoCoMo — benchmarks are self-reported with incompatible methodologies and should not be used as differentiators
evidence: D5b contradiction warning: Ogham 91.8% LongMemEval, Graphiti 18.5% gain, Memori 81.95% LoCoMo. Different benchmarks, different methodologies, all self-reported by tool authors

claim: MemoryMesh's schema-driven auto-tool-generation (define entity types as YAML, get MCP tools) is a generalizable pattern worth adopting for T28 regardless of backend
evidence: D5b finding: MemoryMesh generates CRUD MCP tools from YAML entity type definitions. Pattern reduces boilerplate for multi-node-type graphs

claim: Harper Reed's Claude Code -> Obsidian node pipeline (March 2026) is a working external proof-of-concept of T28's exact write path
evidence: D2a serendipity finding: Claude Code parses sources, writes to Obsidian nodes via wiki-links. Working pipeline as of March 2026. Validates the files-as-nodes approach externally

claim: bobmatnyc/kuzu-memory uses a ryugraph fork rather than LadybugDB — the kuzu MCP ecosystem is fragmented across forks
evidence: D5a finding: kuzu-memory (22 stars) uses ryugraph fork. MemoryGraph (D5b, 202 stars) supports LadybugDB directly. Two separate fork paths exist in the kuzu MCP ecosystem

claim: Obsidian at T28 scale (1,000 nodes) performs cleanly; built-in graph view bottlenecks at 3,000-6,000 densely-linked nodes — above that, use external graph engines
evidence: D2a finding: editor/API confirmed clean at 10,000+ notes, graph view renderer bottlenecks at 3,000-6,000. At T28's 1,000 nodes everything works. Scale ceiling is relevant for JASON-OS

claim: MAGMA's orthogonal graph decomposition (4 separate graphs: temporal, causal, semantic, entity over same node set) enables intent-aware retrieval — research prototype, no production implementation
evidence: arXiv:2601.03236. 45.5% reasoning accuracy gain, 95% token reduction claimed (research prototype). Intent-aware: 'Why?' routes to causal graph, 'When?' to temporal graph
